CONTENT.Club career: Akpa Akpro made his Ligue 1 debut with Toulouse during the 2011–12 season. On 18 April 2014, Akpa Akpro signed a new three-year deal with the le Téfécé side, keeping him contracted until June 2017.On 14 February 2018, he was signed by Italian team Salernitana on a six-month contract.On 1 September 2020, Akpa Akpro joined Lazio on a two-year deal. He scored his first goal for the club on 20 October 2020, in a 3–1 Champions League victory over Borussia Dortmund.On 1 September 2022, Akpa Akpro was loaned to Empoli. On 26 August 2023, Akpa Akpro signed for Serie A side Monza on a one-year loan with an option for purchase.
CONTENT.Personal life: Akpa Akpro's brothers Jean-Jacques and Jean-Louis are also both footballers.
CONTENT.External links: Jean-Daniel Akpa Akpro – French league stats at LFP – also available in French (archived)Jean-Daniel Akpa Akpro at Soccerway
CONTENT.Career statistics: As of match played 12 July 2024As of match played 6 September 2021
CONTENT.International career: From 2013, Akpa Akpro began to be called by Ivory Coast for official matches, making his debut in a 3–0 win against Gambia.He represented the side at the 2014 FIFA World Cup.

SUMMARY: Jean-Daniel Dave Lewis Akpa Akpro (born 11 October 1992) is a professional footballer who plays as a midfielder for Serie A club Lazio. Born in France, he plays for the Ivory Coast national team.
